content:
Is this the only such email you have? In other words, what to other messages with a JPG attachment look like? What about other image formats (PNG, GIF, BMP, etc.)?

If it's just that one message, I would consider it to be somehow corrupt and move on. You might be able to Save the attachments to your local drive and view them outside of Thunderbird with Picture Manager.

If all messages with images, try going to menu item <Settings | General | Files & Attachments> and change the Action for JPG to use some image viewer OTHER THAN Picture Manager. Then re-start Thunderbird, check and see of the message in question uses that other viewer properly, then go back and change the Action back to your preferred Picture Manager.

Is it better?

content:
The answer is "Yes" unless they do something drastically wrong. If you want to be extra careful, copy all messages to Local Folders, and backup your Profile for good measure. After the account is back on line and you're satisfied that everything is intact, you can delete the Local copies.

It's a good idea to make regular backups of your Thunderbird Profile,

content:
Unless you have the Reply window visible and having the focus and accepting your typing, your message is likely to be nowhere. It would depend on what specific keys you hit. You might accidentally make Thunderbird do something sensible, like save a draft (Ctrl+S), but you're more probably going to trigger Thunderbird to do something you don't intend.

Check out the standard keyboard shortcuts for Thunderbird and you might see some of the possible actions:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/keyboard-shortcuts-thunderbird

content:
Hello there Jaya jay.


Imap Are you seeing greyed-out sections? This can happen if Thunderbird limits concurrent connections to prevent server instability. What account protocols (IMAP/POP) are you using?

Try to use the local storage trick: right-click on the local folders > select new folder in that present pane > give it a temporary name > go to the source account and click on all messages and choose 'copy to local folder' > new local folder. Always copy first then move it.

Note If this process fails >don t worry you won t lose data.

Once you have copied all data safely to that new local folder, it's time to go to the other account, create a new folder there too, copy all data into that, and move it to new folders / folder from there.

Add-on issue If you have issues creating folders, it could be caused by bad add-ons. Try using troubleshooting mode to identify them, then update or remove them.


Try Restart your computer and app to fix temporary bugs. If issues persist, try repairing the parent folder, ensuring there is enough free space (inbox too) and that the folder is not overfull.

Select the parent folder if you want to use the repair folder.

Tip You cannot add sub-folders to a mailbox .

Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird


Can you get through this process?

content:
Fixed it, apparently solution was to just use the following settings: Incoming: Protocol IMAP Hostname outlook.office365.com Port 993 Connection security SSL/TLS Authentication method OAuth2 And have the username be my edu email Outgoing: Protocol SMTP Hostname smtp.office365.com Port 587 Connection security STARTTLS Authentication method OAuth2 And same as before, username is edu email

Hope this helps anyone else having the same information
creator: linaskrulikauskas

content:
Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)
creator: @next
